instruction: Fix the `snapshot_check_hint()` function in R/reporter-check.R to only emit the snapshot download hint when running in a GitHub Actions job named 'R-CMD-check'. Currently, the hint is emitted in any GitHub Actions environment, but it should only appear when the job name matches 'R-CMD-check' since that's where the testthat artifact is typically uploaded. Also update the documentation for `snapshot_download_gh()` to clarify that `dest_dir` is the 'Package root directory' rather than just 'Directory to download to', and note that the hint is only emitted in R-CMD-check jobs.
context: The `snapshot_check_hint()` function in R/reporter-check.R currently checks `if (on_gh())` to determine if it should emit a hint. The fix requires adding an additional condition to check `Sys.getenv("GITHUB_JOB") == "R-CMD-check"`. The test in tests/testthat/test-reporter-check.R uses `withr::local_envvar()` to set up GitHub Actions environment variables for testing.

fail_to_pass: test_that("generates informative snapshot hints", { ... }) - the test should set GITHUB_JOB="R-CMD-check" and verify the hint is generated correctly
pass_to_pass: Existing tests for snapshot_check_hint() when not on GitHub Actions should still pass
Tests where GITHUB_JOB is set to something other than "R-CMD-check" should not emit the hint

key_changes: Modify the condition in snapshot_check_hint() from `if (on_gh())` to `if (on_gh() && Sys.getenv("GITHUB_JOB") == "R-CMD-check")`
Update the @param dest_dir documentation from 'Directory to download to' to 'Package root directory'
Add documentation note that the hint is only emitted in R-CMD-check jobs
Update the test to include GITHUB_JOB = "R-CMD-check" in the environment variables
potential_pitfalls: Forgetting to update the test to include the new GITHUB_JOB environment variable
Using incorrect environment variable name or job name comparison
Not updating the .Rd documentation file (man/snapshot_download_gh.Rd) after changing roxygen comments
solution.reference_diff:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index 9effac55e..510e4273b 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 # testthat 3.3.1
 
+* The hint to use `snapshot_download_gh()` is now only emitted when running in a job named "R-CMD-check" (#2300).
 * `expect_snapshot_file()` correctly reports file name if duplicated (@MichaelChirico, #2296).
 * Fixed support for `shinytest2::AppDriver$expect_values()` screenshot snapshot failing on CI (#2293, #2288).
 
diff --git a/R/reporter-check.R b/R/reporter-check.R
index 1d5ac7d51..77c680ecb 100644
--- a/R/reporter-check.R
+++ b/R/reporter-check.R
@@ -123,7 +123,7 @@ summary_line <- function(n_fail, n_warn, n_skip, n_pass) {
 snapshot_check_hint <- function() {
   intro <- "To review and process snapshots locally:"
 
-  if (on_gh()) {
+  if (on_gh() && Sys.getenv("GITHUB_JOB") == "R-CMD-check") {
     repository <- Sys.getenv("GITHUB_REPOSITORY")
     run_id <- Sys.getenv("GITHUB_RUN_ID")
 
diff --git a/R/snapshot-github.R b/R/snapshot-github.R
index 74e278a11..ae160bffc 100644
--- a/R/snapshot-github.R
+++ b/R/snapshot-github.R
@@ -7,11 +7,13 @@
 #' take the artifacts from.
 #'
 #' Note that you should not generally need to use this function manually;
-#' instead copy and paste from the hint emitted on GitHub.
+#' instead copy and paste from the hint emitted on GitHub. This hint is only
+#' emitted when running in a job named "R-CMD-check", since that's where the
+#' testthat artifact is typically uploaded.
 #'
 #' @param repository Repository owner/name, e.g. `"r-lib/testthat"`.
 #' @param run_id Run ID, e.g. `"47905180716"`. You can find this in the action url.
-#' @param dest_dir Directory to download to. Defaults to the current directory.
+#' @param dest_dir Package root directory. Defaults to the current directory.
 #' @export
 snapshot_download_gh <- function(repository, run_id, dest_dir = ".") {
   check_string(repository)
diff --git a/man/snapshot_download_gh.Rd b/man/snapshot_download_gh.Rd
index 23e5999f1..38b85a7f3 100644
--- a/man/snapshot_download_gh.Rd
+++ b/man/snapshot_download_gh.Rd
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ snapshot_download_gh(repository, run_id, dest_dir = ".")
 
 \item{run_id}{Run ID, e.g. \code{"47905180716"}. You can find this in the action url.}
 
-\item{dest_dir}{Directory to download to. Defaults to the current directory.}
+\item{dest_dir}{Package root directory. Defaults to the current directory.}
 }
 \description{
 If your snapshots fail on GitHub, it can be a pain to figure out exactly
@@ -20,5 +20,7 @@ easy, only requiring you to interactively select which job you want to
 take the artifacts from.
 
 Note that you should not generally need to use this function manually;
-instead copy and paste from the hint emitted on GitHub.
+instead copy and paste from the hint emitted on GitHub. This hint is only
+emitted when running in a job named "R-CMD-check", since that's where the
+testthat artifact is typically uploaded.
 }
